diagrams.title: Kruskal's Algorithm Process
diagrams.description: Shows how Kruskal's algorithm builds MST by adding edges in increasing order, merging components using Union-Find
elements: Cycle check: when edge connects same component
diagrams.purpose: Understand edge-by-edge greedy construction and cycle detection

diagrams.title: Prim's Algorithm Growth
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how Prim's algorithm grows a single tree from a starting vertex, always extending to the closest unvisited vertex
diagrams.purpose: Visualize tree-based growth and minimum key selection

diagrams.title: Cut Property Illustration
diagrams.description: Visual representation of the Cut Property showing a cut dividing vertices into two sets and the minimum crossing edge
elements: Graph with vertices in two groups
Minimum weight edge crossing cut (must be in MST)
Other crossing edges shown for comparison
diagrams.purpose: Understand the fundamental property underlying greedy MST algorithms

diagrams.title: Cycle Property Illustration
diagrams.description: Shows a cycle in graph and demonstrates why the maximum weight edge in a cycle cannot be in MST
elements: Cycle with edges of different weights
diagrams.purpose: Understand why cycle edges are excluded from MST construction
